assertions.title: Invalid credentials stay simple and retryable

steps.observed: Login form displayed with email, password, and company code fields
Alert shows 'Invalid credentials', form remains editable, URL stays at /login

steps.action: Verify form fields are enabled after error

assertions.title: Lockout shows lockout-specific feedback

steps.action: Attempt multiple failed logins with validation.admin@example.com / MOCK
steps.observed: Made 9+ failed login attempts, all returned AUTH_001 invalid credentials error

assertions.issues: Cannot trigger lockout state - backend lockout threshold appears to be higher than 9 attempts, or the test account has special lockout settings. Frontend code review confirms lockout UI is implemented (lockout banner, disabled submit button) but backend did not return AUTH_005 during testing.

assertions.title: Login-time tenant runtime denials show stable non-credential messaging
assertions.issues: Cannot test tenant runtime denials (TENANT_ON_HOLD, TENANT_BLOCKED, TENANT_REQUEST_RATE_EXCEEDED) without backend state changes. These require the tenant to be put on hold, blocked, or rate-limited by an administrator - not something that can be triggered from the login UI. Frontend code review confirms runtime denial handling is implemented with distinct messaging.

assertions.title: MFA-required login moves into challenge state from the canonical login contract

steps.observed: Successfully redirected to /mfa showing 'Two-factor verification' page with 6-digit code input

evidence.network: POST /api/v1/auth/login -> 428 with AUTH_007 (MFA required), followed by redirect to /mfa

blockers.description: VAL-AUTH-002 (lockout): Could not trigger lockout state after 9+ failed login attempts. Backend lockout threshold is unknown and may be configured higher than our test attempts, or the validation test accounts may have special lockout policies.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Made additional failed login attempts but never received AUTH_005 response

blockers.description: VAL-AUTH-003 (tenant runtime denials): Cannot test without backend administrator action to put tenant in HOLD, BLOCKED, or rate-limited state.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: None - requires backend state change outside test scope

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-AUTH-001, VAL-AUTH-004), 2 blocked (VAL-AUTH-002, VAL-AUTH-003). Invalid credentials correctly show retryable message and keep form editable. MFA-enabled account correctly redirects to challenge state. Lockout and tenant denial states could not be triggered through the UI alone.
